name: Azure AI Agents MCP Server
what_it_does: Official Microsoft Azure AI Agents MCP server enabling AI agents to create and manage Azure AI Foundry agent runs, access Azure AI services (GPT-4o, DALL-E, embeddings), manage agent threads and conversations, and integrate Azure's enterprise AI infrastructure into MCP-based agentic workflows. Provides Azure-native agent orchestration through the MCP protocol.
use_cases: Creating and running Azure AI Foundry agents from orchestration agents
Accessing Azure OpenAI models (GPT-4o, GPT-4 Turbo) from Azure-native agents
Managing agent conversation threads from multi-turn dialogue agents
Integrating Azure AI Search for RAG workflows from knowledge retrieval agents
Running Azure AI code interpreter for data analysis from analytics agents
Orchestrating enterprise Azure AI pipelines from enterprise workflow agents
not_for: Teams not using Azure (use AWS Bedrock, Google Vertex, or Anthropic MCPs for other clouds)
Lightweight AI integration (Azure AI Agents is enterprise-grade with associated complexity)
best_when: An enterprise team using Azure cloud needs to integrate Azure AI Foundry agents and Azure OpenAI into MCP-based workflows — official Microsoft MCP with enterprise-grade Azure AI infrastructure.
avoid_when: You use AWS, Google Cloud, or prefer direct model APIs — Azure AI Agents adds Azure-specific complexity and cost.

auth.notes: Azure AD authentication (managed identity or service principal) recommended for production. API key for development. Configure AZURE_OPENAI_ENDPOINT and AZURE_OPENAI_KEY or use DefaultAzureCredential for managed identity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Azure free tier: limited Azure OpenAI credits; AI Foundry has free tier with quotas
pricing.paid_tiers: Azure OpenAI pay-as-you-go; enterprise agreements for committed use
pricing.notes: Azure subscription required. Azure OpenAI costs per token. Azure AI Agents: per agent run + model costs. Monitor costs carefully in agentic loops.

performance.rate_limits: Azure OpenAI: TPM (tokens per minute) and RPM (requests per minute) quotas by model; configurable

known_agent_gotchas: Azure AI Foundry quotas per region and model — request quota increases for production workloads
Azure AD auth setup (managed identity, app registration) is complex — allow setup time
Agent threads have retention periods — long-running workflows may lose thread history
Official Azure-Samples MCP — Microsoft-maintained, enterprise-grade reliability
Cost management critical: agent runs bill per model call + orchestration overhead
Azure region selection affects latency and model availability — select closest region to users
agent_readiness.security_notes: Enterprise-grade Azure security. Azure AD managed identity recommended (no credentials in code). Data residency configurable. FedRAMP High for US government. All Azure compliance certifications. Official Microsoft MCP.

security_score_components.security_notes: Azure enterprise security. Managed identity for zero-secret auth. Configurable data residency. SOC2/ISO27001/HIPAA/FedRAMP compliant. Official Microsoft MCP.
